title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the government is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

title: Germany news: Stade attack victims yet to be identified
summary: Police have yet to identify the six victims of a deadly shooting in the northern German city. Meanwhile, Germany's national men's football coach pledged to stay on the job after an early World Cup exit. DW has more.

title: Germany: At least 6 killed in shooting in Stade
summary: Police said the deadly Stade shooting was likely an "extended family tragedy" triggered by a custody dispute, with the state interior minister describing it as "extremely cold-blooded act of violence."

title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
summary: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private investors.

title: In Germany, political fallout from heat wave begins
summary: Following a major heat wave, critics say the government needs to do more to adapt to climate change. What can policymakers do to ensure the country is better prepared for extreme temperatures in the future?

title: Angela Merkel unveils official portrait in Berlin
summary: On Tuesday former German Chancellor Merkel presented the painting for the official portrait gallery of the country's heads of government. She had previously inspired many artists.

title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.
